items.title: FWIW it’s fairly impossible to try to project what the Hawks do at 57.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
FWIW it’s fairly impossible to try to project what the Hawks do at 57.

When you get that deep in R2, some agents are begging teams NOT to take their guys so they can be priority free agents.

Do the Hawks want to roster a third rookie?

Stash candidates?

Anyway, it’s wide open.

items.title: Did you realize the Spurs haven’t fired a coach or a general manager in 30 years? That kind of continuity is unthinkable in pro sports, and it’s one reason Brian Wright can take big draft swings other GMs can’t.
items.author: Mike Finger
items.body_excerpt:
Did you realize the Spurs haven’t fired a coach or a general manager in 30 years? That kind of continuity is unthinkable in pro sports, and it’s one reason Brian Wright can take big draft swings other GMs can’t.

On patience as a team-building superpower:

www.expressnews.com/sports/spurs...

media.title: Why patience is a team-building superpower for Brian Wright, Spurs
media.description: Brian Wright will be calling the shots in the Spurs’ front office for years to come, which enables him to take the kind of draft night swings many of his fellow general managers can’t.

"I learned a lot throughout this whole process, talking with them at the combine. And then when I came and worked out here, I got to spend, you know, a good 3 days here...

items.title: Keaton Wagler said that he watches several NBA guards in the league, but mentioned Tyrese Haliburton and Shai Gilgeous-Alexander as the two he really tends to take after. Wagler said “closest person is probably Haliburton” for his play style.

items.title: Rockets Big Board: Top Draft Options at No. 53

items.body_excerpt: Rockets Big Board: Top Draft Options at No. 53 Sports Illustrated

items.title: The Miami Heat have exercised their $2.30 million team option on Pelle Larsson for the 2026-27 season. Larsson appeared in 70 games (54 starts) last season, averaging 11.4 points, 3.5 rebounds, and 3.4 assists. He shot 49.6 percent from the field.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: The Miami Heat have exercised their $2.30 million team option on Pelle Larsson for the 2026-27 season. Larsson appeared in 70 games (54 starts) last season, averaging 11.4 points, 3.5 rebounds, and 3.4 assists. He shot 49.6 percent from the field.
